ucs.title: [LLM] Windigo / Ebury — trojanized libkeyutils.so or OpenSSH binary tampering on Linux servers
ucs.description: Hunts for the canonical Ebury (Windigo G0124) persistence primitive: replacement of the libkeyutils.so shared library, which OpenSSH transitively links to via DT_NEEDED, or in-place modification of /usr/sbin/sshd / ssh / ssh-add / scp. ESET's Operation Windigo and CERN's 2019 Ebury reports both fingerprint these specific files as the trojan landing site, with libkeyutils.so growing from ~10–12 KB legit to 50–150 KB when carrying the Ebury implant.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_size) as file_size values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash values(Filesystem.process_name) as initiating_proc values(Filesystem.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified","renamed") AND ( (Filesystem.file_name="libkeyutils.so*" AND Filesystem.file_size>20000) OR Filesystem.file_path IN ("/usr/sbin/sshd","/usr/bin/ssh","/usr/bin/ssh-add","/usr/bin/scp","/usr/libexec/openssh/sftp-server","/usr/lib/openssh/sftp-server") OR Filesystem.file_name IN ("libns2.so","libns5.so","libsbr.so") ) AND NOT Filesystem.process_name IN ("dpkg","apt","apt-get","yum","dnf","rpm","zypper","pacman","unattended-upgrade","update-alternatives","snapd") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.action | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel — same detection via ASIM ImFileEvent (cross-vendor: MDE, AuditD, Sysmon-for-Linux)
let _pkg_managers = dynamic(["dpkg","apt","apt-get","yum","dnf","rpm","zypper","pacman","unattended-upgrade","update-alternatives","snapd"]);
let _ebury_alt_libs  = dynamic(["libns2.so","libns5.so","libsbr.so","libpamc.so.0"]);
let _openssh_targets = dynamic(["/usr/sbin/sshd","/usr/bin/ssh","/usr/bin/ssh-add","/usr/bin/scp","/usr/libexec/openssh/sftp-server","/usr/lib/openssh/sftp-server"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where ActingProcessName !in~ (_pkg_managers)
| where ( TargetFileName startswith "libkeyutils.so" and TargetFileSize > 20000 )
     or ( TargetFilePath in~ (_openssh_targets) )
     or ( TargetFileName in~ (_ebury_alt_libs) and TargetFilePath matches regex @"(?i)^/(usr/)?lib(64|x32)?(/|$)" )
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, EventType, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName,
          TargetFileSize, TargetFileSHA256, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine,
          ActorUsername
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Windigo Ebury Trojanized libkeyutils or OpenSSH Binary Modification
id: 7c2b8d8e-5a8c-4a2f-9e0b-6d3a1c4b9a01
status: experimental
description: Detects creation or modification of /lib*/libkeyutils.so* with abnormal size (>20KB), or in-place modification of OpenSSH binaries (sshd, ssh, ssh-add, scp, sftp-server) outside of package-manager context — the canonical Ebury / Windigo (G0124) install footprint.
references:
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2014/03/18/operation-windigo-the-vivisection-of-a-large-linux-server-side-credential-stealing-malware-campaign/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0124/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/software/S0377/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1554
  - attack.t1574
  - attack.t1014
  - g0124
logsource:
  product: linux
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection_libkeyutils:
    TargetFilename|startswith:
      - '/lib/libkeyutils.so'
      - '/lib64/libkeyutils.so'
      - '/usr/lib/libkeyutils.so'
      - '/usr/lib64/libkeyutils.so'
  selection_openssh_targets:
    TargetFilename:
      - '/usr/sbin/sshd'
      - '/usr/bin/ssh'
      - '/usr/bin/ssh-add'
      - '/usr/bin/scp'
      - '/usr/libexec/openssh/sftp-server'
      - '/usr/lib/openssh/sftp-server'
  filter_pkg_mgr:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/dpkg'
      - '/apt'
      - '/apt-get'
      - '/yum'
      - '/dnf'
      - '/rpm'
      - '/zypper'
      - '/pacman'
      - '/unattended-upgrade'
      - '/update-alternatives'
      - '/snapd'
  condition: (selection_libkeyutils or selection_openssh_targets) and not filter_pkg_mgr
falsepositives:
  - Manual administrator rebuild of OpenSSH from source (rare in production)
  - Out-of-band patch deployment via Ansible/Salt running as root without invoking the package manager
level: high
ucs.rationale: This isn't a generic 'unsigned binary modification' rule — it pins on the EXACT three artefacts ESET and CERN documented as the Ebury install signature: (1) oversized libkeyutils.so used as a DT_NEEDED hijack against sshd, (2) in-place patching of the OpenSSH binaries themselves, and (3) Ebury's known alternate drop names (libns2/libns5/libsbr/libpamc). The 20KB lower bound on libkeyutils is the t
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.

ucs.title: [LLM] Windigo / Ebury — sshd-initiated DNS exfiltration of stolen SSH credentials (long-label TXT/A queries)
ucs.description: Hunts the Ebury credential-exfil channel: the in-memory implant inside the trojanized sshd hooks PAM/keyboard-interactive auth, captures plaintext SSH credentials and private keys, then exfiltrates them via DNS queries (A/TXT) with base64-encoded payloads in the leftmost labels to attacker-controlled nameservers. CERN's 2019 Ebury post-mortem documents this as the persistent C2 method even after the 2014 ESET disclosure.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(DNS.query) as queries dc(DNS.query) as unique_queries values(DNS.record_type) as record_types min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS where DNS.src_category!="workstation" AND DNS.process_name="sshd" by host DNS.dest DNS.query_type | `drop_dm_object_name("DNS")` | eval long_label_count=mvcount(mvfilter(match(queries,"^[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{30,}\."))) | where long_label_count>3 OR unique_queries>20 | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="sshd" All_Traffic.dest_port=53 NOT All_Traffic.dest_category="internal_dns" by host All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("All_Traffic")` ] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Windigo / Ebury — sshd talking DNS to the public internet, OR sshd issuing
// long-label DNS queries (the Ebury credential-exfil channel).
let _lookback = 7d;
// Path A: sshd opens UDP/53 directly to a public resolver — sshd should never
// do this; it always goes through libc/getaddrinfo → systemd-resolved/nscd.
let PathA = DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "sshd"
  | where RemotePort == 53
  | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
  | project Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl,
            InitiatingProcessCommandLine, Channel = "sshd_direct_dns_egress";
// Path B: long-label DNS queries by sshd — Ebury's base64 payload tell.
let PathB = DeviceEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
  | where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "sshd"
  | extend Q = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName)
  | extend FirstLabel = tostring(split(Q, ".")[0])
  | extend LabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel),
           TotalLen = strlen(Q),
           B64ish   = iff(FirstLabel matches regex @"^[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]+$", true, false)
  | where (LabelLen >= 30 and B64ish) or TotalLen >= 100
  | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Q, FirstLabel, LabelLen, TotalLen,
            InitiatingProcessCommandLine, RemoteIP = "", RemotePort = 53,
            RemoteUrl = Q, Channel = "sshd_long_label_dns";
union isfuzzy=true PathA, PathB
| summarize Hits = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            SampleQuery = any(RemoteUrl),
            Destinations = make_set(RemoteIP, 25)
            by DeviceName, Channel
| where Hits > 0
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel — Path A via ImNetworkSession (sshd-on-public-DNS) and Path B via
// ImDnsActivity (long-label payloads). Either alone is suspicious; both is Ebury.
let _lookback = 7d;
let PathA = ImNetworkSession
  | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
  | where ActingProcessName =~ "sshd"
  | where DstPortNumber == 53
  | where not(ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr))
  | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url,
            ActingProcessCommandLine, Channel = "sshd_direct_dns_egress";
let PathB = ImDnsActivity
  | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
  | where SrcUsername has "sshd" or DvcHostname != ""   // Im* doesn't always carry process; rely on Syslog enrichment
  | extend FirstLabel = tostring(split(DnsQuery, ".")[0])
  | extend LabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel),
           TotalLen = strlen(DnsQuery),
           B64ish   = iff(FirstLabel matches regex @"^[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]+$", true, false)
  | where (LabelLen >= 30 and B64ish) or TotalLen >= 100
  | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, DnsQuery, FirstLabel, LabelLen, TotalLen,
            DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber = 53, Url = DnsQuery,
            ActingProcessCommandLine = "", Channel = "sshd_long_label_dns";
union isfuzzy=true PathA, PathB
| summarize Hits = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            SampleQuery = any(Url),
            Destinations = make_set(DstIpAddr, 25)
            by DvcHostname, Channel
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Generic 'DNS-tunnel' rules misfire on McAfee/Symantec query strings, AV cloud lookups, and CDN tokens. This rule is Windigo-specific because it anchors on the PROCESS — sshd — which on a clean Linux host should never make DNS queries directly (it relies on libc → systemd-resolved/nscd). Any sshd→public-UDP/53 traffic is by itself anomalous, and ESET's Operation Windigo writeup plus CERN's 2019 fol

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImDnsActivity, ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
